metadata.title: Guideline on the Clinical Investigation of Human Plasma Derived Von Willebrand Factor Products

rules.rule_text: A pharmacokinetic trial for new VWF products must include at least 12 subjects with severe VWD (baseline VWF:RCo < 15-20%), with at least 6 having hereditary type 3 VWD.
rules.verbatim_quote: A pharmacokinetic trial should be performed in at least 12 subjects suffering from severe VWD defined here as baseline VWF:RCo < 15-20%. At least 6 out of 12 patients should be suffering from hereditary type 3 VWD for separate analysis.
rules.exceptions: Due to the rareness of hereditary type 3 vWD (prevalence between 0.55 to 6.0 per million) applicants might encounter difficulties to enrol 6 patients with type 3 vWD. In this case the competent authority should be contacted.

rules.rule_text: Patients in the PK trial must be at least 12 years of age, must not have received DDAVP or VWF-containing product for at least 5 days prior, must be without inhibitors to FVIII and VWF, and must not be actively bleeding.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients should be at least 12 years of age. They should not have received an infusion of DDAVP or of a VWF containing product for at least five days prior to participation in the trial. The study should be performed in patients without inhibitors to FVIII and VWF, who are not actively bleeding.

rules.rule_text: The PK trial must measure both VWF and FVIII parameters including incremental recovery, in vivo half-life, AUC, Cmax, tmax, clearance, and mean residence time (MRT), analyzed in a centralised coagulation laboratory.
rules.verbatim_quote: The pharmacokinetic trial should include measurements of VWF as well as FVIII. The following parameters should be recorded: incremental recovery, in vivo half-life, area under the curve (AUC), Cmax, tmax, clearance and mean residence time (MRT). Analysis of these parameters should be performed in a centralised laboratory specialised in coagulation.

rules.rule_text: PK samples for VWF:RCo, VWF:Ag, VWF:CB, and FVIII:C must be taken before infusion of 80 VWF:RCo IU/kg and at specified timepoints (15 min, 1, 3, 6, 12, 24, 30, 48, and 72 hours post-infusion). At least three different lots must be used.
rules.verbatim_quote: Samples for determination of VWF:RCo, VWF:Ag, VWF:CB, and FVIII:C should be taken before infusion of 80 VWF:RCo IU/kg of the product and at 15 minutes, 1, 3, 6, 12, 24, 30, 48 and 72 hours after the infusion. At least three different lots should be employed in the trial.
rules.exceptions: It is anticipated that some deviation from the recommendation may occur in clinical practice.

rules.rule_text: Incremental recovery must be determined using the peak level recorded within 3 hours after infusion and reported as [IU/ml]/[IU/kg].
rules.verbatim_quote: Incremental recovery is determined using the peak level recorded within 3 hours after the infusion and reported as [IU/ml]/[IU/kg].
rules.exceptions: In case of a VWF product almost depleted of FVIII, any incremental recovery of FVIII is not expected. However, in all instances FVIII:C should be monitored after the infusion.

rules.rule_text: VWF multimer distribution must be investigated in type 3 VWD patients using samples obtained before, 15 minutes, and 6 hours after infusion, using low- and high-resolution electrophoresis gels (preferably 1% and >2%).
rules.verbatim_quote: The samples from all type 3 patients obtained before 15 minutes and 6 hours after the infusion should be used to investigate the multimer distribution of the VWF by multimeric sizing analysis using low- and high resolution electrophoresis gels (preferably 1% and > 2% gels). These analyses should be performed in a centralised laboratory specialised in VWD phenotyping.

rules.rule_text: Type 3 VWD patients in the PK study must be re-tested for the same PK parameters after at least 6 months using the same dose as the initial investigation.
rules.verbatim_quote: Type 3 VWD patients should be re-tested for the same pharmacokinetic parameters after at least 6 months using the same dose as in the first investigation.

rules.rule_text: Clinical efficacy must be evaluated in at least 20 patients with severe VWD, preferably including all PK study patients. All patients must be at least 12 years old with an observation period of at least 12 months.
rules.verbatim_quote: Clinical efficacy of the VWF product should be evaluated in at least 20 patients, suffering from severe VWD (for definition of severe VWD please refer to 2.1.1) including preferably all the patients who participated in the pharmacokinetic trial. All patients should be at least 12 years of age. During an observation period of at least 12 months any event (spontaneous bleeding, trauma, surgery, labour) should be documented in detail.

rules.rule_text: At least 15 surgical procedures (10 of which major) must be available for efficacy assessment in the clinical trial.
rules.verbatim_quote: At least 15 surgical procedures, 10 of which are considered major, should be available for assessment of efficacy.

rules.rule_text: At least 10 bleeding episodes (with at least 5 major mucosal) must be documented in the efficacy trial.
rules.verbatim_quote: at least 10 bleeding episodes with at least 5 major mucosal bleeding episodes should be documented.

rules.rule_text: Clinical response to treatment must be assessed using a 4-point scale: 'none', 'moderate', 'good', or 'excellent' by the physician for major bleeds.
rules.verbatim_quote: Response should be assessed as 'none', 'moderate', 'good' or 'excellent' by the physician for those patients who receive treatment with the product for major bleeds.
rules.exceptions: In the remaining cases clinical efficacy after administration should be assessed from the clinical response as reported by the patients.

rules.rule_text: If prophylaxis is claimed, a minimum of 5 type 3 VWD patients must be followed for 1 year for absence of spontaneous bleeding episodes or obvious reduction in haemorrhagic manifestations.
rules.verbatim_quote: If prophylaxis is claimed, a minimum of 5 patients with type 3 VWD should be followed for a year for the absence of spontaneous bleeding episodes or obvious reduction of the number of haemorrhagic manifestations.

rules.rule_text: Clinical efficacy must also be assessed by calculating VWF and FVIII consumption expressed as number of infusions, IU/kg per event (on-demand and surgery), or IU/kg per month or year for prophylactic use.
rules.verbatim_quote: Clinical efficacy should also be assessed by calculating the consumption of vWF and FVIII (administered as part of the VWF product or administered as a separate infusion in case of a VWF product depleted of FVIII), expressed as the number of infusions as well as IU/kg per event (on-demand and surgery), or IU/kg per month or year, in case of prophylactic use.

rules.rule_text: All adverse events must be recorded and reported in accordance with ICH E3 guideline on structure and content of clinical study reports.
rules.verbatim_quote: All adverse events should be recorded and reported in accordance with the ICH guideline "structure and content of clinical study reports" (CPMP/ICH/137/95E3)

rules.rule_text: Anti-VWF inhibitor testing must be performed at a centralised laboratory. The assay used to detect inhibitors must be described and justified.
rules.verbatim_quote: the detection and measurement of inhibiting antibodies to VWF should be performed at a centralised laboratory. The assay applied to detect inhibitors should be described and justified.

rules.rule_text: All patients must be tested for inhibitors to VWF and FVIII before entering the PK study or efficacy/safety trial, and re-tested every 3 months until the end of the 1-year observation period.
rules.verbatim_quote: Before entering the pharmacokinetic study or the subsequent efficacy/safety trial, all patients should be tested for inhibitors to VWF and FVIII. All patients should be re-tested every three months until the end of the observation period of 1 year. Patients should always be tested if they are suspected of having developed inhibitors (reduced in vivo recovery, poor clinical response).
rules.exceptions: Additional testing required if inhibitor development is suspected based on reduced in vivo recovery or poor clinical response.

rules.rule_text: For patients developing inhibitors, a full clinical report must be provided including clinical relevance, cumulative incidence, exposure days, antibody titre, and method used.
rules.verbatim_quote: For all patients who develop inhibitors a full clinical report should be provided including clinical relevance, the cumulative incidence and the number of exposure days. The titre of the antibody should be reported and the method used should be specified.

rules.rule_text: Blood samples from patients suspected of or confirmed to have developed inhibitors must be stored at -70 degrees C for possible future inhibitor and DNA testing.
rules.verbatim_quote: Blood samples of patients who are suspected of inhibitors or who have developed inhibitors should be stored at -70°C for possible future inhibitor and DNA testing.

rules.rule_text: A pre-treatment sample from each patient in clinical trials must be stored at -70 degrees C for possible future viral safety testing.
rules.verbatim_quote: A pre-treatment sample from each patient included in the clinical trials should be stored at -70°C for possible future testing.

rules.rule_text: Thrombogenicity testing is relevant for products containing FVIII, especially those with FVIII/VWF ratio >= 1.0 where high FVIII:C levels can be reached with repeated administration. Clinical evaluation of thrombosis must be performed in all surgical patients.
rules.verbatim_quote: This is relevant for products containing FVIII in addition to VWF and especially for products with a ratio of FVIII/VWF ≥ 1.0 in situations of repeated administration as high FVIII:C levels can be reached. Clinical evaluation of thrombosis should be performed by safe objective means in all patients undergoing surgical procedures.
rules.exceptions: Only relevant for products containing FVIII

rules.rule_text: Paediatric efficacy trial must include at least 8 children under 6 years with severe VWD (VWF:RCo <15-20%), with at least 3 having hereditary type 3 VWD, regardless of prior treatment.
rules.verbatim_quote: an open multi-centre efficacy trial should include at least 8 children with VWD under the age of six years suffering from severe VWD defined as VWF:RCo<15-20%, regardless of prior treatment. Three of these should be suffering from hereditary type 3 VWD.

rules.rule_text: The paediatric trial must not start until interim data of 1 year of exposure are available in 10 patients older than 12 years included in the efficacy trial. The study must continue until patients have been treated for 1 year.
rules.verbatim_quote: The trial should not be started until interim data of 1 year of exposure are available in 10 patients older than 12 years, who are included in the efficacy trial. The study should continue until the patients have been treated for one year.

rules.rule_text: Until a paediatric study has been completed, the SPC must include a statement that there is no clinical data to characterise the response in children less than 6 years of age.
rules.verbatim_quote: Until such a study has been carried out, the SPC should include a statement that there is no data from a clinical study to characterise the response to use of the product in children less than 6 years of age.
rules.exceptions: Removed once paediatric study is completed and SPC is updated.

rules.rule_text: A post-marketing study protocol must be submitted with the dossier, designed based on efficacy/safety trial results, to ensure long-term consistency between clinical study data and routine use data.
rules.verbatim_quote: To ensure consistency in the long-term between data from the clinical studies and from routine use, a post-marketing study should be undertaken and a protocol submitted with the dossier. The results of the efficacy/safety trial should be taken into account in the design of this study.

rules.rule_text: For modified VWF products, a comparative cross-over PK trial must be performed with the same minimum requirements as for new products (12 subjects, at least 6 type 3 VWD).
rules.verbatim_quote: A comparative cross-over pharmacokinetic trial should be performed in at least 12 subjects suffering from severe VWD defined here as baseline VWF:RCo < 15-20 %. At least 6 out of 12 patients should be suffering from hereditary type 3 VWD for separate analysis.
rules.exceptions: Same exception for difficulty enrolling type 3 patients applies.

rules.rule_text: For modified products, evidence must demonstrate that manufacturing process changes have not affected PK. If significant impact on coagulation factor activity cannot be excluded, full PK, efficacy, and safety data must be provided.
rules.verbatim_quote: If significant impact on the activity of the coagulation factor cannot be excluded, data on pharmacokinetics, efficacy and safety should also be provided with the application.
cross_references: EMEA/CHMP/3097/02 Guideline on comparability of biotechnology derived proteins

rules.rule_text: All patients entering clinical trials must be vaccinated against hepatitis A and B (unless protective antibodies are present from prior infection), with evidence of immunisation at baseline.
rules.verbatim_quote: According to good clinical practice all patients entered into clinical trials should be vaccinated against hepatitis A and B (unless protective antibodies are present resulting from a previously experienced infection), and evidence of immunisation should be present at baseline.
rules.exceptions: Not required if protective antibodies already present from previous infection.

rules.rule_text: Clinical trials must be performed according to ICH Note for Guidance on Good Clinical Practice.
rules.verbatim_quote: The clinical trials described in this Guideline should be performed according to the ICH Note for Guidance on Good Clinical Practice (CPMP/ICH/135/95).
